summary: The paper supports a bounded handoff mechanism for many robustness failures and a pathway-specific repair, but not a general causal account of all failures.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Popular code-generation multi-agent systems were not robust to semantic-preserving input mutations, failing on 7.9% to 83.3% of previously solved problems.
bounded_claims.bounds: This is a descriptive robustness result under the tested mutation operators, systems, models, datasets, and Pass@10 setup; it establishes loss of robustness but not a single causal explanation for all observed failures.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The repair method appears much better at addressing planner-coder-gap failures than invalid cases, indicating a bounded rather than general fix.
bounded_claims.bounds: The strongest subtype evidence comes from a specific reported setting, so the claim is best read as a bounded pathway match between the intervention and planner-coder-gap failures, not as a universal cross-setting rule.
